data.regime: suppressed — significant central bank influence keeping yields low
implications: Financial repression — negative real returns on safe assets favor equities
Credit markets benefit from yield suppression
data.methodology: Yardeni Bond Vigilantes Model (coined 1983): 10Y Treasury yield vs. Nominal GDP YoY growth. When yield exceeds GDP growth, bond vigilantes are enforcing fiscal discipline. When yield is suppressed below GDP growth, central bank policy dominates.

vanna_charm_regime.interpretation: VIX rising rapidly — dealers adding hedges creating selling pressure. Vanna+charm flows negative for equities.

data.regime: Anomalous regime — intermarket relationships breaking down

dow_theory.confirmation: CONFIRMED_BEARISH — both averages in downtrend (Dow Theory sell signal)

historical_analogues.what_followed: 20% SPX drawdown (Sep-Dec 2018), Fed pivot to cuts in 2019
historical_analogues.lesson: Tight labor + Fed hiking + credit widening = equity correction. Fed pivot was the catalyst for recovery.

historical_analogues.what_followed: 2022 bear market (-25% SPX), worst bond year in decades, Fed hiked aggressively
historical_analogues.lesson: Ultra-tight spreads + rising inflation + accommodative Fed = mispriced risk. Inflation was not transitory.

historical_analogues.what_followed: Narrow AI-driven rally, broadening concerns, rate cut debate, carry trade unwind (Aug 2024)
historical_analogues.lesson: Narrow markets with sticky inflation and high rates are fragile. Concentration risk + vol compression = sharp corrections on catalysts.

so_what_chains.because: Sticky services inflation + potential commodity passthrough from energy prices
so_what_chains.so_what: Fed stays hawkish → real yields stay elevated → P/E compression risk for growth stocks
so_what_chains.portfolio_action: Favor value over growth, add inflation hedges (TIPS, commodities), reduce duration

so_what_chains.because: Risk repricing — either fundamental deterioration or liquidity withdrawal
so_what_chains.so_what: Credit leads equity by 2-3 months. Widening spreads precede earnings downgrades and equity selloffs.
so_what_chains.portfolio_action: Reduce HY and leveraged loan exposure. Move up in quality. Add Treasury allocation.

so_what_chains.because: ISM decomposition suggests weakening new orders and/or rising inventories
so_what_chains.so_what: Earnings growth will decelerate. Forward guidance likely to disappoint. Cyclicals most exposed.
so_what_chains.portfolio_action: Defensive rotation: Healthcare > Discretionary, Staples > Cyclicals, Quality > Momentum

fixed_income: Credit spreads elevated — avoid HY, favor IG or Treasuries
Inflation elevated — favor TIPS over nominals, short duration
recommendations.conviction_note: Some uncertainty. Reduce position sizes by 30-50%.

data.executive_summary: Growth: contraction, Inflation: hot, Credit: elevated. Stress: 4.0/10, Late-cycle: 4/13. Closest analogue: Q4 2018 (Fed overtightening) (50% match). Conviction: MODERATE.

position_sizing.sizing_rule: Wider stop -> reduce position size. Tighter stop -> can size up.

trailing_rules.trailing_method: 1:1 trailing -- trail stop by 1 unit for every 1 unit of profit above breakeven

risk_management_rules: Cut before stop if thesis deteriorates
Tighten all stops if macro regime shifts to risk-off
Re-enter at different level if stopped out but thesis intact
Manage correlated positions -- don't double up on same factor
data.asset_specific_notes: Gold: 4H RSI oversold is historically a strong buy signal. Avoid buying when 4H RSI overbought. Stops typically 30-160 points below entry. Half size on wide stops.

fidenza_framework.rule_1: Risk 0.75-2.0% of capital per trade (0.7-1.0% for ES swings)
fidenza_framework.rule_2: Stop = recent swing low/high (1-3 days prior)
fidenza_framework.rule_3: Position size inversely proportional to stop width
fidenza_framework.rule_4: Trail stop to breakeven after 1R profit
fidenza_framework.rule_5: Cut before stop when thesis deteriorates
fidenza_framework.rule_6: Tighten all stops during regime shift to risk-off
fidenza_framework.rule_9: Re-enter after stops if thesis intact
fidenza_framework.rule_10: Adapt sizing to volatility regime -- higher vol = wider stop + smaller size

data.summary: Gold long at $3,348.00. Recommended stop: $3,297.78 (percent-based, 1.5% risk). Trail to breakeven after $50.22 profit. Risk 1.5% of capital per trade.
